description:
You are a sustainability engineer preparing the carbon footprint of a standard concrete masonry unit (CMU, 17.2 kg). Build a complete cradle-to-gate life cycle model in OpenLCA.

Create a new database 'CMU_Footprint'. Model four unit processes with the exact flows and quantities below:

'Cement Production': output 1 kg 'Portland Cement' (reference product flow); inputs 1.52 kg 'Limestone', 0.092 kg 'Coal' (product flows); elementary emission output 0.83 kg 'Carbon Dioxide' to air (representing calcination).

'Aggregate Processing': output 1 kg 'Mixed Aggregate' (reference product flow); inputs 1.05 kg 'Raw Gravel', 0.003 kg 'Diesel Fuel' (product flows).

'Concrete Mixing': output 1 kg 'Fresh Concrete' (reference product flow); inputs 0.15 kg 'Portland Cement', 0.75 kg 'Mixed Aggregate', 0.10 kg 'Process Water' (product flows).

'Block Molding': output 17.2 kg 'CMU Block' (reference product flow); inputs 17.2 kg 'Fresh Concrete', 0.021 kg 'Diesel Fuel' (product flows).

Create a product system 'CMU_Production_System' with 'Block Molding' as reference process. Link all upstream processes: Cement Production and Aggregate Processing feed into Concrete Mixing, which feeds Block Molding.

Import LCIA methods from ~/LCA_Imports/lcia_methods.zip. Calculate Global Warming Potential using TRACI 2.1 and export results to ~/LCA_Results/cmu_footprint.csv.
